title: Synsepalum msolo
intro: Synsepalum dulcificum, commonly known as the miracle fruit or Synsepalum msolo, is a plant species native to West Africa that contains unique compounds capable of altering the taste of sour substances. The health benefits of Synsepalum dulcificum include its potential to aid in weight loss, reduce inflammation, and improve digestion, due in part to its ability to modulate the body's response to sweet and sour tastes. The bioactive constituents of this plant include miraculin, a glycoprotein responsible for its sweetening properties, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and alkaloids. Synsepalum dulcificum is often used to make herbal preparations such as tablets, powders, and extracts, which can be used to create a temporary, taste-altering effect that may aid in the management of sweet cravings.

This family includes over 150 species of trees and shrubs native to tropical and subtropical regions. Synsepalum msolo is native to tropical Africa and is a relatively small tree species.
